title: Fix-first: onboard parity check follows the two-projection layout (Claude settings vs claude-hooks.json)
action: Integration repair after the codex-parity-2 catalog inversion (commit d1777ed): skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs (line ~475) hard-codes hooks/hooks.json as the plugin catalog for its Claude-settings parity check, so it now compares Claude repo settings against the CODEX projection and fails on the intentionally-absent PreToolUse::Agent|Task::bee-model-guard.mjs triple. Update the parity check to the two-projection layout: Claude repo settings compare against hooks/claude-hooks.json; add (or keep) an assertion that hooks/hooks.json (Codex projection) differs from the Claude projection ONLY by the allowed differences exported from hooks/catalog.mjs. Change comparison wiring only — never drop or weaken the parity assertion itself.

truths: the parity check compares Claude repo settings against hooks/claude-hooks.json and passes on the current tree
the allowed-differences boundary (bee-model-guard Claude-only) is asserted from hooks/catalog.mjs ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES, not re-hardcoded
no other onboard check is modified
prohibitions: no weakening of the parity assertion
no edits to hooks/ or templates/

trace.outcome: Rewired the onboard parity check to the two-projection layout after the codex-parity-2 catalog inversion: Claude repo settings now compare against hooks/claude-hooks.json (restores the pass), and a new assertion compares hooks/hooks.json (Codex) against hooks/claude-hooks.json (Claude), requiring every diff triple to be explained by hooks/catalog.mjs's ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES export (imported, never re-hardcoded) and every ALLOWED_DIFFERENCES entry to correspond to a real diff.
